question: Which project identity, folder-binding and bounded lifecycle semantics should M02 make authoritative?
context: The accepted M01 implementation derives a project identifier from its current folder path. That was sufficient for one local conversation but is unsafe for M02: moving a folder can create a second logical identity, while reusing a path can accidentally inherit the wrong history or trust. M02 also needs explicit semantics for optional portable .dennett metadata and shared project memory, ordinary non-Git folders, imported repository trust and the difference between removing a registration and deleting user files. This decision establishes only the smallest durable boundary needed for create, attach, missing and rebind; archive, clone, transfer, remote publication and destructive deletion remain deferred.

options.description: Keep path-derived identity and treat each moved folder as a new project
benefits: requires the smallest immediate schema and migration change
costs: project history and trust cannot follow an intentional folder move
a reused path can be confused with an older logical project
later provider, device and memory work must replace the identity model
risks: silent history split or unsafe trust reuse after filesystem changes

options.description: Use a stable generated Project ID, optional portable .dennett metadata and separate relocatable local WorkspaceBindings
benefits: project identity survives folder moves and supports later devices and providers
shared project memory and identity can travel with a repository while personal memory, chats, secrets and access policy remain local
filesystem location and availability remain explicit observations without treating ordinary source changes as trust changes
remove registration can be safe by default because source deletion is a separate unsupported operation
costs: introduces the first project registry migration and explicit rebind flow
requires a clear offer to create the minimal .dennett structure without silently modifying imported folders
risks: an incomplete migration could strand an M01 path-derived project unless compatibility is tested
portable metadata can be copied or edited, so project files must never grant or expand local permissions

reason: Stable logical identity plus a separate local binding matches the canonical ownership model, prevents path reuse from becoming authority, and is the smallest design that remains valid when Dennett later adds more providers or devices. Portable .dennett metadata carries only common project identity and memory; the local Dennett profile remains authoritative for personal memory, chats, secrets and a simple project_id-to-policy mapping. Ordinary source changes do not revoke trust, and project files may request but never grant authority. Unknown imports begin Restricted; normal execution requires Trusted-Bounded authority; plain folders use bounded before-image checkpoints unless Git is explicitly initialized; M02 never deletes source files or publishes remotely.

resolution.note: The owner approved stable generated Project IDs and relocatable local WorkspaceBindings with these refinements: .dennett/project metadata and .dennett/memory are optional portable, Git-friendly project state shared by all recipients; personal memory, chats, secrets, provider and agent choices, and access policy remain in the local Dennett profile; local permissions use the simple project_id-to-policy mapping and are never granted by project files; ordinary project changes do not trigger trust friction; a folder without .dennett still receives a stable local ID and the add-existing-folder flow explicitly offers, but never silently performs, creation of the minimal .dennett structure.
